summary: Fixed a bad merge in src/melee/ft/fighter.h by restoring and reorganizing fighter-related header definitions. The change added missing ftCommonData and FtCollisionData layout declarations near the top of the header, expanded ftData with collision data and additional pointer/field slots, moved Vec2/Vec3 typedefs after ftData, restored extra early fields in CollData at offsets 0x40 and 0x44, and removed a duplicate/minimal ftCommonData definition from the bottom of the file. Evidence is limited to the PR title/body and diff; there were no review comments.
what_changed: Repaired fighter.h after a bad merge, according to the PR title and author note.
Added a fuller ftCommonData declaration near the top of the header, annotated as pointing to data in PiCo.dat.
ftCommonData now includes explicit filler ranges and named fields at offsets 0x480, 0x498, 0x5F0, 0x768, 0x76C, 0x770, and 0x774.
Added FtCollisionData with filler through 0x28 and field x28.
Expanded ftData to include FtCollisionData* x4C_collisionData, filler_x50, and void* x58, replacing the previous shorter filler-only layout around 0x4C.
Moved typedef struct _Vec2 { float x, y; } Vec2 and typedef Vec Vec3 after ftData rather than before it.
Reformatted ThrowFlags and FighterBone indentation without apparent semantic changes.
Moved CollData below CameraBox and restored/addded early fields x40 and x44 before the existing ECB correction and wall/environment fields.
Changed CollData x134_envFlags from int to u32.
Removed the older minimal trailing ftCommonData definition that only covered filler to 0x768 and fields x768 through x774.
smart_moves: Kept struct layout offset comments and filler-size expressions such as 0x498-0x484 and 0xB4-0x48, which helps preserve ABI-sensitive offsets while improving known field coverage.
Avoided leaving two competing ftCommonData definitions in the same header by replacing the old bottom-of-file minimal definition with a more complete declaration near related data definitions.
Introduced a named FtCollisionData pointer in ftData at x4C instead of treating that region only as filler, making the header more reusable for future fighter and collision decompilation.
Preserved unknown fields with offset-based names rather than overclaiming semantics.
decomp_lessons: When repairing merge damage in shared decomp headers, verify that struct declarations are not duplicated and that type ordering still satisfies downstream use.
For partially understood game data, keep exact offset-based filler arrays and comments so later work can add fields without shifting established layout.
Prefer introducing small placeholder structs such as FtCollisionData when a pointer target is known but the full structure is still mostly unknown.
Header merge fixes can be high-impact even with only one changed file because fighter.h defines central layouts used across fighter code.
naming_conventions: Unknown or partially understood fields use offset-prefixed names such as x480, x498_ledgeCooldownTime, x5F0, x768, x76C, x770, x774, x40, and x44.

Filler arrays use offset/range-oriented names such as data_filler_1, data_filler_2, data_filler_3, filler_x5F4, filler_x0, filler_x48, filler_x50, filler_xBC, filler_x134, filler_x160, and filler_x174.
Struct typedefs retain project-style names with leading underscore tags, for example struct _ftCommonData, struct _FtCollisionData, struct _ftData, struct _Vec2, and struct _CollData.

Known semantic fields combine offset prefixes with descriptive names, such as x498_ledgeCooldownTime, x4C_collisionData, xB4_ecbCurrCorrect_right, xBC_ecbCurrCorrect_left, x134_envFlags, x160_rightwall_index, and x174_leftwall_index.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff uses explicit offset-preserving filler arrays to maintain binary-compatible struct layouts, a common matching tactic for decompilation headers.
No assembly diff, function matching discussion, or reviewer-provided matching tactic was present in the slice.
The use of typed pointers and offset comments suggests the fix was intended to make C field accesses line up with known data offsets, but the PR slice does not provide direct match evidence.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR context.
The only human text in the PR body was: "my b".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify ftCommonData in src/melee/ft/fighter.h.
Find later PRs that expand FtCollisionData or rename x4C_collisionData.
Search for uses of x498_ledgeCooldownTime to confirm semantics.
Search for CollData x40 and x44 to see whether they were later named.
Compare fighter.h after PR #297 with adjacent PRs to understand what bad merge was corrected.
